homepage.text_excerpt: --> Home | Paul Foster MP top of page Home About In Parliament Latest Contact Help and Support Fostering change for South Ribble in Parliament About Paul Foster Read more about Paul Foster MP How can I help Helping with individual issues, supporting our community, and working on policy Get in touch Find out how to get in touch with me Welcome Welcome to my website. As your Member of Parliament, I’m elected to be your voice in Parliament and your champion in South Ribble. ​ ​ Since my election on 4th July 2024, my team has helped over 11000 people with casework. If you need my help, please do not hesitate to get in touch . For more information on the kind of issues I can help with, please click here . Every week I receive hundreds of emails with questions and requests for support. I always try to respond to these as quickly as possible, but I have to prioritise personal questions from constituents over automated campaign e-mails, though every issue raised with me is read and taken seriously. In the Constituency In Parliament Policy Work Campaigns Latest Unfolding Situation in the Middle East Letter: Pause of ASD and ADHD support for children and young people Appointment to serve the Defence Select Committee for the Armed Forces Bill Runshaw Colleges' Question Time Paul Foster Member of Parliament South Ribble Subscribe to my newsletter Email * Next I want to subscribe to your mailing list. paul.foster.mp@parliament.uk 01772 364003 Unit 1a, Balfour Court, Leyland, Lancashire PR25 2TF bottom of page

pages.title: About | Paul Foster MP
pages.meta_description: Official website of Paul Foster MP. Member of Parliament for the South Ribble constituency.
pages.text_excerpt: --> About | Paul Foster MP top of page Home About In Parliament Latest Contact Help and Support Paul Foster Member of Parliament South Ribble Phone: 01772 364003 Email: paul.foster.mp@parliament.uk Address: Unit 1a, Balfour Court, Leyland, Lancashire PR25 2TF Bio I am honoured to serve as the Member of Parliament for South Ribble since July 2024. Before stepping into this role, I had the privilege of leading South Ribble Borough Council for five years, where I worked tirelessly for our community and navigated the Council through the challenges of the Covid crisis. I was raised in Barrow-in-Furness. My journey into public service began unexpectedly during the shipyard strikes of 1988, which led me to join the British Army’s Corps of Royal Engineers at the age of 18. Following a highly successful military career, I served operationally in Iraq (1992) and Kosovo (1999 and 2001), as well as tours of duty in Germany, Gibraltar, Cyprus, Belize, and the Falkland Islands, with a short stint Afghanistan in 2002. I retired from the army in 2003 and made South Ribble my home, where I’ve lived for over two decades. After retiring from the military, I worked as a Construction Project Manager in various schemes around the North-West of England. I was later appointed Project Director for an international renewable energy organisation, overseeing projects in the UK, Poland, Lithuania, South Africa, Spain, and Mexico. Following this, I served as Project Director for the Port of Tyne in the North-East before establishing my own Project Management & Development Consultancy, delivering renewable energy, social housing, and student accommodation throughout the UK. Politically, I was first elected as a local Councillor in 2007 on South Ribble Borough Council, a seat I successfully held for five elections over 17 years. I was elected the Leader of the South Ribble Labour Group in 2015 and became the Leader of the Council in 2019, a position I held until being elected the Member of Parliament for South Ribble in July 2024. ​ My commitment to our community has always been my driving force, whether as a councillor or now as your MP. I focus on issues related to defence and local government, need new road infrastructure to lessen congestion and travel times and improve air quality, areas where I have considerable expertise. I hope to be a strong constituency MP, ensuring that the residents of South Ribble receive the support they need. ​ In my maiden speech, I highlighted the unique characteristics and challenges of South Ribble, reflecting the concerns of our community. I emphasised my commitment to public ownership of passenger railway services, the need for more quality housing and developments in the right places so that communities and families can continue to live together. Representing South Ribble is a great honour, and I am committed to using my experience in the military, industry, and local government to serve our community effectively. I look forward to continuing my work in Parliament, always prioritising the needs of our constituents. Timeline May 2007 - July 2024 May 2019 - July 2024 July 2024 Councillor - South Ribble Borough Council Leader - South Ribble Council Member of Parliament - South Ribble Social Media Paul Foster Member of Parliament South Ribble Subscribe to my newsletter Email * Next I want to subscribe to your mailing list. paul.foster.mp@parliament.uk 01772 364003 Unit 1a, Balfour Court, Leyland, Lancashire PR25 2TF bottom of page

headings: h2: Letter: Pause of ASD and ADHD support for children and young people
pages.text_excerpt: --> In Parliament | Paul Foster MP top of page Home About In Parliament Latest Contact Help and Support In Parliament Here, you can follow my work in the House of Commons and Parliament, where I tirelessly advocates for our community's interests and works on legislation that shapes our nation's future. From participating in debates and committees to presenting bills and raising important issues. Parliamentary Contributions Parliamentary News Letter: Pause of ASD and ADHD support for children and young people The decision by Lancashire and South Cumbria NHS Foundation Trust to pause new referrals and halt progress on existing waiting lists for autism (ASD) and ADHD assessments for children and young people is deeply concerning to me. I share this concern with other Lancashire-based MPs, so I have joined them in writing to the Trust to raise our concerns. Paul Foster National Heart Month February is 'Heart Month' - a time when we shine a light on cardiovascular disease and the everyday steps that can reduce risk, like healthy eating and exercise. Paul Foster Government’s new Troubles Bill As a veteran, I’ve met far too many people who have been personally affected by the previous Government’s unlawful Legacy Act - families who were effectively shut out of the search for truth and justice. Paul Foster 1 2 3 4 5 Paul Foster Member of Parliament South Ribble Subscribe to my newsletter Email * Next I want to subscribe to your mailing list. paul.foster.mp@parliament.uk 01772 364003 Unit 1a, Balfour Court, Leyland, Lancashire PR25 2TF bottom of page

h2: Appointment to serve the Defence Select Committee for the Armed Forces Bill
pages.text_excerpt: --> Latest | Paul Foster MP top of page Home About In Parliament Latest Contact Help and Support All Posts Campaigns In Parliament In the Constituency Policy Work Unfolding Situation in the Middle East People across South Ribble have been in touch with me over the past few days regarding the situation in Iran and the wider Middle East. I know that people are worried, especially those with family or friends in the region. Paul Foster Mar 6 1 min read Letter: Pause of ASD and ADHD support for children and young people The decision by Lancashire and South Cumbria NHS Foundation Trust to pause new referrals and halt progress on existing waiting lists for autism (ASD) and ADHD assessments for children and young people is deeply concerning to me. I share this concern with other Lancashire-based MPs, so I have joined them in writing to the Trust to raise our concerns. Paul Foster Feb 17 2 min read Appointment to serve the Defence Select Committee for the Armed Forces Bill I’m honoured to have been appointed to sit on the Select Committee scrutinising the new Armed Forces Bill, especially because it's a Bill I care deeply about. It matters profoundly to me because I understand first-hand what is at stake, and it’s a responsibility I will take extremely seriously. Paul Foster Feb 16 2 min read Runshaw Colleges' Question Time Thank you to the students at Runshaw College for the invitation to take part in their college Question Time panel last week. There was plenty of thoughtful discussion and lively debate. It’s genuinely refreshing to see so many politically engaged young people, and it’s fantastic that they’re given a platform to have their voices heard. Paul Foster Feb 6 1 min read Paul Foster Member of Parliament South Ribble Subscribe to my newsletter Email * Next I want to subscribe to your mailing list. paul.foster.mp@parliament.uk 01772 364003 Unit 1a, Balfour Court, Leyland, Lancashire PR25 2TF bottom of page

pages.title: Help and Support | Paul Foster MP
pages.meta_description: Official website of Paul Foster MP. Member of Parliament for the South Ribble constituency.
pages.text_excerpt: --> Help and Support | Paul Foster MP top of page Home About In Parliament Latest Contact Help and Support Help and Support Your Voice, Our Community, A Better Future. Welcome to the official website of Paul Foster MP, your dedicated representative for South Ribble. Paul is committed to serving the community with integrity, compassion, and a deep sense of responsibility. Whether you're seeking assistance with individual concerns, looking to strengthen our local community, or interested in the policy work Paul is involved in, this site is here to provide you with the information and resources you need. Helping with Individual Issues Paul Foster MP is dedicated to assisting constituents with a wide range of individual issues. Whether you're facing challenges with government services, need help navigating bureaucratic processes, or have personal concerns that require attention, Paul is here to help. His office can provide guidance, support, and advocacy to ensure your voice is heard and your issues are addressed. ​ Supporting Our Community Paul Foster MP is committed to the well-being and development of the South Ribble community. He actively engages with local organizations, attends community events, and works closely with residents to understand and address their needs. From supporting local businesses to championing community projects, Paul is dedicated to making South Ribble a better place for everyone. Working on Policy ​ As your representative in Parliament, Paul Foster MP is involved in shaping policies that impact our community and the nation. He participates in debates, votes on legislation, and serves on committees to ensure that the interests of South Ribble are represented. Paul is particularly passionate about issues such as healthcare, education, and social justice, and he works tirelessly to advocate for policies that benefit all constituents. Paul Foster Member of Parliament South Ribble Subscribe to my newsletter Email * Next I want to subscribe to your mailing list. paul.foster.mp@parliament.uk 01772 364003 Unit 1a, Balfour Court, Leyland, Lancashire PR25 2TF bottom of page

headings: h2: Letter: The Humanitarian Catastrophe in Gaza
pages.text_excerpt: --> Campaigns | Paul Foster MP top of page Home About In Parliament Latest Contact Help and Support Campaigns Campaigning on things that matter to South Ribble Welcome to the campaigns page of Paul Foster MP! Here, you can stay updated on Paul's ongoing efforts to make a positive impact in our community of South Ribble and beyond. From advocating for better healthcare services to championing environmental sustainability, Paul is dedicated to addressing the issues that matter most to you. Troubles, Tragedy & Trauma It was a real honour to meet Michael, and to see how, out of unimaginable loss, a powerful legacy has been created in Majella’s memory through ‘Troubles, Tragedy & Trauma’. Paul Foster Letter: The Humanitarian Catastrophe in Gaza As a dad, I cannot begin to imagine the horror of watching your child starve or be caught in conflict while simply trying to find food. What we are witnessing in Gaza is utterly inhumane. My constituents across South Ribble have written to me in their hundreds, demanding action and I agree with them. I’ve written to the Foreign Secretary urging immediate steps to protect civilians, ensure aid is delivered, and support a lasting path to peace. Paul Foster 1 2 3 4 Paul Foster Member of Parliament South Ribble Subscribe to my newsletter Email * Next I want to subscribe to your mailing list. paul.foster.mp@parliament.uk 01772 364003 Unit 1a, Balfour Court, Leyland, Lancashire PR25 2TF bottom of page
